title: Survivors
content:
No doubt they'll soon get well; the shock and strain
     Have caused their stammering, disconnected talk.
    Of course they're "longing to go out again," - 
     These boys with old, scared faces, learning to walk,
    They'll soon forget their haunted nights; their cowed
     Subjection to the ghosts of friends who died, - 
    Their dreams that drip with murder; and they'll be proud
     Of glorious war that shatter'd all their pride ...
    Men who went out to battle, grim and glad;
    Children, with eyes that hate you, broken and mad.

     CRAIGLOCKART,
        Oct. 1917.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Siegfried Loraine Sassoon, titled "Survivors", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
